digest.component_or_area: PyPI release workflow (release.yml) authorization and publication gate
propositions: Publication is currently gated only on pushed v* tags with no check that the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing an arbitrary tag to publish.
The release should be initiated via workflow_dispatch from main so the source is reviewed by construction, replacing the tag-push publish trigger.
Version values across pyproject.toml, server.json top-level, and packages[].version are kept in lockstep only by a manual runbook, risking mismatched published versions with no machine check.
A version-lockstep job and a git merge-base ancestry assertion must run before build and fail on mismatch or non-ancestor SHA.
Publishing goes directly via PyPA Trusted Publishing OIDC (no tag relay, no PAT), gated on a pypi environment re-keyed to main-via-dispatch with required reviewers.
A release-time preflight queries the pypi environment API and fails closed if required reviewers or a main-only branch policy are missing, detecting drift when it matters.
